neighborhoods.name: Downtown Fredericksburg
neighborhoods.description: Fredericksburg's historic core along the Rappahannock River. Walkable to restaurants, boutiques, the Amtrak station, and the VRE. Popular with young professionals and empty nesters who want a city feel without DC prices.
neighborhoods.commute_to_dc: 55–75 minutes via I-95 or VRE train (~70 min)
highlights: Walkable downtown with shops and restaurants
Amtrak and VRE commuter rail access
No car needed for daily errands
neighborhoods.best_for: Professionals, commuters, history lovers, empty nesters

neighborhoods.name: Celebrate Virginia / Rappahannock Landing
neighborhoods.description: One of the fastest-growing areas in Spotsylvania, just south of the city. Large newer homes, resort-style amenities, and strong school ratings. Very popular with families relocating from Northern Virginia.
highlights: Community pools, trails, and sports courts
neighborhoods.best_for: Families, buyers relocating from NOVA, new construction seekers

neighborhoods.name: Embrey Mill
neighborhoods.description: Stafford's premier master-planned community. Award-winning design with a town center, pools, dog parks, trails, and a brewery. Consistently ranks among the top communities in the region for family living.
highlights: Town center with shops and dining
Miles of walking and biking trails
Close to Quantico and Fort Gregg-Adams corridor
neighborhoods.best_for: Families, military families, professionals wanting amenities

neighborhoods.name: Aquia Harbour
neighborhoods.description: A gated waterfront community in northern Stafford with a private marina, golf course, horse stables, and a landing strip. One of the most unique communities in the region — feels like a resort while being 40 miles from DC.
neighborhoods.best_for: Boaters, golfers, pilots, those seeking resort lifestyle

neighborhoods.name: Fawn Lake
neighborhoods.description: Spotsylvania's most prestigious gated community built around a 288-acre private lake. Arnold Palmer-designed golf course, country club, beach, tennis, and boating. The region's premier address for luxury buyers.
highlights: 288-acre private lake for boating and fishing
Arnold Palmer golf course and country club
Luxury custom homes on large lots
neighborhoods.best_for: Luxury buyers, lake lifestyle, golf enthusiasts, retirees

neighborhoods.name: North Stafford / Widewater
neighborhoods.description: The most affordable area in Stafford with easy I-95 access to Quantico and DC. Popular with first-time buyers and military families. Mix of established subdivisions and newer townhome communities.
highlights: Mix of single-family homes and townhomes
neighborhoods.best_for: First-time buyers, military families, investors, budget-conscious commuters

neighborhoods.name: Lake Anna
neighborhoods.description: A 17,000-acre reservoir about 35 miles southwest of Fredericksburg. Virginia's most popular inland lake for recreational use. Strong demand for vacation homes, primary residences, and short-term rental investment properties. Two sides: private (residential) and public (recreation).
highlights: Private-side homes have deeded water access
neighborhoods.best_for: Vacation home buyers, Airbnb investors, retirees, water enthusiasts

neighborhoods.name: Leeland Station / Central Park Area
neighborhoods.description: Mid-Stafford location within walking distance of the Leeland Road VRE station. Strong appeal for DC and Pentagon commuters who want a suburban home and an easy train ride. Central Park shopping center nearby for all daily needs.
highlights: Walking distance to VRE commuter rail
No I-95 stress for DC commuters
Strong resale value due to VRE access
